example.print_categories.documentation:
## Summary:
Prints the categorized titles and their associated metadata from a Wikipedia page in alphabetical order.

## Description:
This function extracts category information from a Wikipedia page object and displays it in a formatted manner. It is designed to be used in Wikipedia data analysis or exploration workflows where category information needs to be displayed to users.

## Args:
    page (wikipediaapi.Page): A Wikipedia page object containing category information. This object must have a `categories` attribute that behaves like a dictionary.

## Returns:
    None: This function does not return any value. It performs I/O operations by printing to standard output.

## Raises:
    AttributeError: If the provided page object does not have a `categories` attribute or if `categories` is not dictionary-like.

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
    - The `page` parameter must be a valid wikipediaapi.Page object
    - The `page.categories` attribute must be accessible and behave like a dictionary with string keys
    
    Postconditions:
    - All category titles from the page are printed to stdout in alphabetical order
    - The original page object remains unmodified

## Side Effects:
    - Prints formatted output to standard output (stdout)
    - No modifications to the input page object or external state

## Control Flow:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Start print_categories] --> B{page.categories exists?}
    B -->|No| C[Raise AttributeError]
    B -->|Yes| D[Get categories dict]
    D --> E[Sort category keys]
    E --> F{Has categories?}
    F -->|No| G[End]
    F -->|Yes| H[Iterate through sorted keys]
    H --> I[Print formatted category info]
    I --> J[Next category]
    J --> F
```

## Examples:
    # Basic usage with a Wikipedia page object
    wiki_wiki = wikipediaapi.Wikipedia('en')
    page = wiki_wiki.page("Python (programming language)")
    print_categories(page)
    # Output would display categories like:
    # Computer programming: Category:Computer programming
    # Programming languages: Category:Programming languages
```

example.print_langlinks.documentation:
## Summary:
Prints language links from a Wikipedia page in a formatted manner.

## Description:
This function extracts and displays language links associated with a Wikipedia page, showing the language code, target language name, page title, and full URL for each link. It's designed to provide a readable output of multilingual references for a given Wikipedia article.

## Args:
    page (wikipediaapi.Page): A Wikipedia page object containing language links data.

## Returns:
    None: This function doesn't return any value; it prints output directly to stdout.

## Raises:
    AttributeError: If the page object doesn't have a langlinks attribute, or if langlinks[key] objects don't have language, title, or fullurl attributes.

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
    - The page parameter must be a valid wikipediaapi.Page object
    - The page object must have a langlinks attribute that behaves like a dictionary
    - Keys in langlinks must be sortable (strings or comparable types)
    - Each langlinks[key] must have language, title, and fullurl attributes
    
    Postconditions:
    - All language links from the page are printed to standard output in alphabetical order by language code

## Side Effects:
    - Prints formatted text to standard output (stdout)
    - No external state mutations or I/O operations beyond printing

## Control Flow:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Start print_langlinks] --> B[Get page.langlinks]
    B --> C[Sort langlinks keys]
    C --> D{Has entries?}
    D -->|Yes| E[Iterate through sorted keys]
    E --> F[Get langlinks[key]]
    F --> G[Print formatted string]
    G --> H[Next key]
    H --> D
    D -->|No| I[End]
```

## Examples:
    # Assuming a wikipediaapi page object exists
    page = wiki_wiki.page("Python (programming language)")
    print_langlinks(page)
    # Output would be something like:
    # de: Deutsch - Python (Programmiersprache): https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(Programmiersprache)
    # es: español - Python: https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python
    # fr: français - Python: https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python

## Summary:
Prints all links from a Wikipedia page in alphabetical order.

## Description:
This function extracts and displays all hyperlinks found on a given Wikipedia page, sorting them alphabetically by title before printing. It serves as a utility for inspecting the internal linking structure of Wikipedia articles.

## Args:
    page: A Wikipedia page object containing a `links` attribute. The page object must have a `links` property that behaves like a dictionary mapping link titles to link targets.

## Returns:
    None: This function does not return any value. It performs I/O operations by printing to standard output.

## Raises:
    AttributeError: If the provided `page` object does not have a `links` attribute.
    TypeError: If `page.links` is not a dictionary-like object with keys() method.

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
    - The `page` parameter must be a valid Wikipedia page object from the wikipediaapi library
    - The `page.links` attribute must be accessible and iterable
    - The keys of `page.links` must be sortable (support comparison operations)
    
    Postconditions:
    - All links from the page are printed to stdout in alphabetical order
    - The original page object remains unmodified

## Side Effects:
    - Prints formatted output to standard output (stdout)
    - No external state mutations or I/O operations beyond printing

## Control Flow:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Start print_links] --> B{page.links exists?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Sort links.keys()]
    C --> D[Iterate through sorted titles]
    D --> E[Print "%s: %s" format]
    E --> F[End]
    B -- No --> G[AttributeError]
    G --> H[End]
```

## Examples:
```python
# Basic usage with a Wikipedia page
wiki_wiki = wikipediaapi.Wikipedia('en')
page = wiki_wiki.page("Python (programming language)")
print_links(page)
# Output would be sorted links like:
# Algorithms: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algorithm
# Array data structure: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Array_data_structure
# ...
```

## Summary:
Removes content delimited by PYPI-BEGIN and PYPI-END markers from documentation text.

## Description:
Processes text to strip out sections marked with PYPI-BEGIN and PYPI-END delimiters, commonly used to exclude certain content from package distribution documentation.

## Args:
    txt (str): Input text that may contain PYPI-BEGIN/PYPI-END marker sections

## Returns:
    str: Text with all content between PYPI-BEGIN and PYPI-END markers removed

## Raises:
    None explicitly raised

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
        - Input must be a string
    Postconditions:
        - All content between matching PYPI-BEGIN and PYPI-END markers is removed
        - Text structure outside marked sections remains unchanged

## Side Effects:
    None

## Control Flow:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Input txt] --> B{Contains markers?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Apply regex substitution]
    B -- No --> D[Return original txt]
    C --> E[Remove marked sections]
    E --> F[Return cleaned text]
    D --> F
```

## Examples:
    >>> fix_doc("Hello .. PYPI-BEGIN world PYPI-END goodbye")
    'Hello  goodbye'
    
    >>> fix_doc("No markers here")
    'No markers here'
    
    >>> fix_doc(".. PYPI-BEGIN start end PYPI-END")
    ''
```

wikipediaapi.__init__.ExtractFormat.documentation:
## Summary:
Enumeration representing different extraction formats for Wikipedia content processing.

## Description:
The ExtractFormat class defines constants for specifying how Wikipedia content should be extracted and processed. It serves as a type-safe way to indicate whether content should be processed in wiki markup format or HTML format, enabling different parsing strategies for various use cases.

This enum is used throughout the wikipediaapi library to control content extraction behavior, particularly when dealing with section parsing and HTML tag handling.

## State:
- WIKI: Integer value 1, enables recognizing subsections in wiki markup format
- HTML: Integer value 2, allows retrieval of HTML tags from content

The enum values are immutable and represent fixed extraction strategies. Both values are integers (1 and 2) as required by IntEnum inheritance.

## Lifecycle:
- Creation: Instantiated automatically when referenced by name (WIKI or HTML)
- Usage: Used as a parameter in functions that require format specification
- Destruction: Managed automatically by Python's garbage collection

## Method Map:
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[ExtractFormat.WIKI] --> B[Wiki Markup Processing]
    A --> C[Section Recognition]
    A --> D[Subsection Parsing]
    
    E[ExtractFormat.HTML] --> F[HTML Tag Processing]
    E --> G[Tag Retrieval]
    E --> H[HTML Content Extraction]
```

## Raises:
No exceptions are raised during instantiation as this is a simple enum definition.

## Example:
```python
# Using the enum values
format_wiki = ExtractFormat.WIKI
format_html = ExtractFormat.HTML

# These can be used as parameters in functions requiring format specification
# e.g., api.page('Article', extract_format=format_wiki)
```

wikipediaapi.__init__.Wikipedia.__init__.documentation:
## Summary:
Initializes a Wikipedia client object with configuration for API requests.

## Description:
Constructs a Wikipedia client that handles communication with Wikipedia's API endpoints. This method validates required parameters and sets up the HTTP session with appropriate headers for making requests to Wikipedia services.

## Args:
    user_agent (str): HTTP User-Agent string required by Wikipedia's terms of service. Must be longer than 5 characters.
    language (str, optional): Language code for the Wikipedia instance (e.g., 'en', 'fr'). Defaults to 'en'.
    extract_format (ExtractFormat, optional): Format specification for content extraction. Defaults to ExtractFormat.WIKI.
    headers (Dict[str, Any], optional): Additional HTTP headers to include in requests. Defaults to None.
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to the underlying requests library.

## Returns:
    None: This method initializes instance attributes and performs setup operations.

## Raises:
    AssertionError: When user_agent is not provided or shorter than 6 characters, or when language is not specified.

## State Changes:
    Attributes READ: None
    Attributes WRITTEN: 
        - self.language: Set to stripped and lowercased language parameter
        - self.extract_format: Set to the provided extract_format parameter
        - self._session: Initialized requests.Session() with configured headers
        - self._request_kwargs: Set to merged kwargs including default timeout

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
        - user_agent must be a non-empty string with length > 5 characters
        - language must be a non-empty string
    Postconditions:
        - self.language is stored as a lowercase, stripped string
        - self._session is initialized with proper headers
        - self._request_kwargs contains all provided kwargs plus default timeout

## Side Effects:
    - Creates a new requests.Session() object
    - Logs initialization information at INFO level
    - Updates default headers with USER_AGENT constant (assumed to be defined elsewhere)

## Summary:
Represents Wikipedia namespace identifiers as integer-enumerated constants.

## Description:
The Namespace class provides a standardized way to reference Wikipedia namespaces throughout the wikipediaapi library. It defines constants for all major Wikipedia namespaces with their corresponding numeric identifiers, making it easier to work with different namespace types without hardcoding magic numbers. This class is used internally by the library to categorize and organize Wikipedia page content according to standard Wikipedia namespace conventions.

## State:
- Inherits from IntEnum, providing integer values for each namespace constant
- Each namespace constant maps to a specific integer identifier as defined by Wikipedia's namespace system
- All constants are immutable once defined
- No instance variables beyond the inherited IntEnum functionality

## Lifecycle:
- Creation: Instantiated automatically when imported; no explicit construction needed
- Usage: Access constants directly as class attributes (e.g., Namespace.MAIN, Namespace.USER)
- Destruction: Managed automatically by Python's garbage collection

## Method Map:

    A --> C[TALK=1]
    A --> D[USER=2]
    A --> E[USER_TALK=3]

    A --> AJ[GADGET_DEFINITION=2302]

```

## Raises:

## Example:

from wikipediaapi import Namespace

user_namespace = Namespace.USER  # Returns 2

    print("This is a user page")

# Iterate over all namespaces
for ns in Namespace:
    print(f"{ns.name}: {ns.value}")
```

wikipediaapi.__init__.Wikipedia.documentation:
## Summary
Wikipedia is a wrapper class for accessing Wikipedia's API, providing methods to retrieve various types of information from Wikipedia pages.

## Description
The Wikipedia class serves as the primary interface for interacting with Wikipedia's API. It handles authentication via User-Agent headers, manages HTTP sessions for efficient request handling, and provides methods to fetch different aspects of Wikipedia pages such as extracts (summaries), page information, language links, links, backlinks, categories, and category members.

This class is designed to be instantiated once and reused for multiple page queries. It maintains a persistent HTTP session for efficiency and follows Wikipedia's User-Agent policy requirements. The class should typically be created once per Wikipedia language edition and reused throughout the application's lifetime.

## State
- language: str - The language code for the Wikipedia instance (e.g., "en", "fr"). Must be non-empty after stripping whitespace.
- extract_format: ExtractFormat - Determines how text extracts are formatted (WIKI or HTML). Default is ExtractFormat.WIKI.
- _session: requests.Session - HTTP session used for making API requests. This session is automatically managed and closed when the object is destroyed.
- _request_kwargs: dict - Additional keyword arguments passed to requests for configuring HTTP behavior such as timeouts, proxies, etc.

## Lifecycle
- Creation: Instantiate with user_agent string and optional language, extract_format, headers, and additional request parameters. The user_agent must comply with Wikipedia's User-Agent policy (minimum 5 characters).
- Usage: Call methods like page(), extracts(), info(), etc. to retrieve information from Wikipedia pages. These methods typically return WikipediaPage objects or data structures containing page information.
- Destruction: Automatically closes the HTTP session when the object is deleted (via __del__). Alternatively, users can manually manage the session lifecycle if needed.

## Method Map
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Wikipedia] --> B[page]
    A --> C[article]
    A --> D[extracts]
    A --> E[info]
    A --> F[langlinks]
    A --> G[links]
    A --> H[backlinks]
    A --> I[categories]
    A --> J[categorymembers]
    B --> K[WikipediaPage]
    D --> L[_query]
    D --> M[_build_extracts]
    E --> L
    E --> N[_build_info]
    F --> L
    F --> O[_build_langlinks]
    G --> L
    G --> P[_build_links]
    H --> L
    H --> Q[_build_backlinks]
    I --> L
    I --> R[_build_categories]
    J --> L
    J --> S[_build_categorymembers]
    L --> T[requests.Session]
```

## Raises
- AssertionError: Raised during initialization if user_agent is missing or too short (less than 5 characters) or if language is empty.
- Various exceptions from requests.Session.get() when making HTTP requests, such as ConnectionError, Timeout, etc.

## Example
```python
import wikipediaapi

# Create a Wikipedia instance for English Wikipedia
wiki = wikipediaapi.Wikipedia('MyApp/1.0', language='en')

# Get a page by title
page = wiki.page('Python_(programming_language)')

# Get page summary with 2 sentences
summary = wiki.extracts(page, exsentences=2)

# Get detailed page information
wiki.info(page)

# Get language links to other language versions
langlinks = wiki.langlinks(page)

# Get links from the page to other Wikipedia pages
links = wiki.links(page)

# Get backlinks (pages that link to this page)
backlinks = wiki.backlinks(page)

# Get categories this page belongs to
categories = wiki.categories(page)

# Get members of a category
category_members = wiki.categorymembers(page)

# Using proxy configuration
wiki_proxy = wikipediaapi.Wikipedia(
    'MyApp/1.0', 
    language='en',
    proxies={'http': 'http://proxy:1234'}
)
```

wikipediaapi.__init__.WikipediaPage.documentation:
## Summary
Represents a Wikipedia page and provides lazy-loaded access to various page properties and metadata through the Wikipedia API.

## Description
The WikipediaPage class serves as a client-side representation of a Wikipedia page. It acts as a proxy that lazily loads data from the Wikipedia API only when specific properties are accessed. This approach minimizes unnecessary API calls and improves performance when working with large amounts of page data.

The class is designed to work with a Wikipedia instance and provides access to various page attributes such as title, language, namespace, summary, sections, links, categories, and more. It implements lazy loading through the `__getattr__` method and `_fetch` helper method, which automatically triggers API calls to retrieve data when needed.

## State
- `wiki`: Wikipedia instance used to make API calls
- `_summary`: String containing the page summary (lazy-loaded)
- `_section`: List of WikipediaPageSection objects representing page sections (lazy-loaded)
- `_section_mapping`: Dictionary mapping section titles to lists of WikipediaPageSection objects (lazy-loaded)
- `_langlinks`: Dictionary of language links to other language versions of the page (lazy-loaded)
- `_links`: Dictionary of links to other Wikipedia pages (lazy-loaded)
- `_backlinks`: Dictionary of pages linking to this page (lazy-loaded)
- `_categories`: Dictionary of categories this page belongs to (lazy-loaded)
- `_categorymembers`: Dictionary of pages belonging to this category (lazy-loaded)
- `_called`: Dictionary tracking which API calls have been made for this page
- `_attributes`: Dictionary storing basic page attributes like title, namespace, language, etc.

## Lifecycle
**Creation**: Instantiate using `Wikipedia.page()` or `Wikipedia.article()` methods, passing the Wikipedia instance, page title, namespace, and language.

**Usage**: Access properties such as `title`, `summary`, `sections`, `links`, etc. These properties trigger lazy loading of data from the Wikipedia API when first accessed. For example, accessing the `sections` property will fetch page data if necessary.

**Destruction**: The class doesn't require explicit cleanup as it holds references to the Wikipedia session, which handles connection management.

## Summary
Fetches data from the Wikipedia API for a specific data category and marks it as retrieved.

## Description
This method serves as a lazy-loading mechanism for fetching Wikipedia page data. When a property access requires data that hasn't been fetched yet, this method is called to retrieve the appropriate data from the Wikipedia API via the `self.wiki` object. It uses dynamic method dispatch to call the appropriate API fetching method based on the `call` parameter.

The method is primarily used internally by the `__getattr__` method and various property getters to ensure data is only fetched when actually needed, implementing a lazy loading pattern for efficient resource usage.

## Args
    call (str): The API call identifier specifying which type of data to fetch. Valid values are:
        - "extracts": Fetches page summary and sections
        - "info": Fetches basic page information like page ID, title, namespace, etc.
        - "langlinks": Fetches language links to pages in other languages
        - "links": Fetches links to other Wikipedia pages
        - "backlinks": Fetches pages that link to this page
        - "categories": Fetches categories this page belongs to
        - "categorymembers": Fetches pages in this category

## Returns
    WikipediaPage: Returns self to enable method chaining

## Raises
    AttributeError: If the specified call method doesn't exist on the `self.wiki` object

## State Changes
    Attributes READ:
        - self.wiki: Used to dynamically call methods via getattr
        - self._called: Used to track which data categories have been fetched
    
    Attributes WRITTEN:
        - self._called[call]: Set to True to mark the specified data category as fetched
        - Various internal attributes of self are populated during the API call processing

## Constraints
    Preconditions:
        - self.wiki must be a valid Wikipedia instance with the requested method
        - call must be one of the recognized API call identifiers
        
    Postconditions:
        - The specified data category is marked as fetched in self._called
        - The corresponding data is populated in the appropriate internal attributes
        - self is returned for method chaining

## Side Effects
    - Makes HTTP requests to the Wikipedia API via self.wiki._query()
    - Modifies internal state by updating self._called
    - Populates various internal data structures (self._summary, self._section, etc.) with fetched data

## Summary:
Implements lazy loading of Wikipedia page attributes by fetching data from the API on first access.

## Description:
This special method is invoked when an attribute is accessed that doesn't exist in the normal attribute lookup. It handles lazy loading of Wikipedia page metadata by mapping attribute names to API calls through the ATTRIBUTES_MAPPING dictionary. The method ensures that API calls are made only once per attribute and caches results for subsequent accesses.

## Args:

## Returns:
    Any: The value of the requested attribute, fetched from the Wikipedia API if necessary

## Raises:
    AttributeError: When the attribute name is not in ATTRIBUTES_MAPPING and cannot be found through normal attribute lookup

## State Changes:
    Attributes READ: self.ATTRIBUTES_MAPPING, self._attributes, self._called
    Attributes WRITTEN: self._attributes (when new values are fetched and cached)

## Constraints:
    Preconditions: The WikipediaPage instance must be properly initialized with ATTRIBUTES_MAPPING, _attributes, and _called dictionaries
    Postconditions: If the attribute exists in ATTRIBUTES_MAPPING, it will be available in self._attributes after the first access

## Side Effects:
    Makes HTTP requests to the Wikipedia API through the wiki object's methods
    Modifies the internal state by updating self._called to mark API calls as completed
    May modify self._attributes by caching fetched values

## Summary:
WikipediaPageSection represents a hierarchical section within a Wikipedia page, containing title, content, and nested subsections.

## Description:
This class models a section of a Wikipedia page with its title, content text, and nesting level. It serves as a node in a tree structure representing the hierarchical organization of Wikipedia articles. Instances are typically created by the Wikipedia class during page parsing operations, particularly through the `_create_section` method which processes extracted text to build section hierarchies.

The class provides methods to navigate subsections by title and to generate formatted text representations of the section and its children, respecting the configured extract format (WIKI or HTML).

## State:
- wiki: Wikipedia instance that owns this section
- _title: str - Title of the section
- _level: int - Indentation/nesting level (0-based)
- _text: str - Content text of the section
- _section: List[WikipediaPageSection] - Child subsections

## Lifecycle:
Creation: Constructed via `WikipediaPageSection(wiki, title, level=0, text="")` with required Wikipedia instance and title parameters.
Usage: Typically accessed through parent section's `sections` property or `section_by_title()` method. The `full_text()` method generates formatted text content including all nested subsections.
Destruction: Managed automatically by Python garbage collection; no explicit cleanup required.

## Method Map:
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[WikipediaPageSection] --> B[title]
    A --> C[level]
    A --> D[text]
    A --> E[sections]
    A --> F[section_by_title]
    A --> G[full_text]
    A --> H[__repr__]
```

## Raises:
- NotImplementedError: When `full_text()` is called with an unknown ExtractFormat value

## Example:
```python
# Create a section manually (rarely done directly)

## Summary
Returns a dictionary mapping language codes to WikipediaPage objects representing translations of the current page in other languages.

## Description
This property provides access to language links (translations) of the current Wikipedia page. It implements a lazy loading pattern where the actual API call to fetch language links is made only when this property is first accessed. The method leverages the underlying Wikipedia API's langlinks module to retrieve cross-language references.

Known callers include:
- Direct property access on WikipediaPage instances
- Internal lazy loading mechanism when accessing page language links

This logic is implemented as a separate property rather than being inlined because it follows the same lazy-loading pattern as other page attributes like links, categories, etc., ensuring efficient resource usage by only fetching data when needed.

## Returns
- Type: PagesDict (dict[str, WikipediaPage])
- Value: Dictionary mapping language codes (e.g., 'fr', 'de') to WikipediaPage objects representing the translated versions of this page
- Edge cases: Returns empty dict if no language links exist or if the page doesn't exist (pageid = -1)

## State Changes
- Attributes READ: self._called["langlinks"], self._langlinks
- Attributes WRITTEN: None (but may modify self._called["langlinks"] when fetching data)

## Constraints
- Preconditions: The WikipediaPage instance must be properly initialized with a valid wiki connection
- Postconditions: After first access, self._called["langlinks"] will be set to True and self._langlinks will contain the language link data

## Side Effects
- Makes an HTTP request to the Wikimedia API endpoint for langlinks
- May trigger additional API calls through the _fetch mechanism if data hasn't been previously loaded

wikipediaapi.__init__.Wikipedia.links.documentation:
## Summary:
Retrieves all links from a Wikipedia page and populates the page's links cache with the results.

## Description:
This method queries the MediaWiki API to fetch all links from a given Wikipedia page using the `query+links` module. It constructs appropriate API parameters, makes the API request, processes the response, and builds a dictionary of linked Wikipedia pages. The method handles pagination for pages with many links and ensures proper caching of results in the page object.

The method is designed as a separate component because it encapsulates the complete workflow for fetching and processing links data from Wikipedia's API, including handling continuation tokens for large datasets and maintaining consistency with the page's attribute system.

Known callers:
- WikipediaPage.links property getter (via _fetch method)
- Direct calls to Wikipedia.links() method

This logic is separated from inline code because it involves complex API interaction, pagination handling, and result building that would clutter the simpler property access pattern.

## Args:
    page (WikipediaPage): The Wikipedia page object from which to retrieve links
    **kwargs: Additional API parameters to customize the query (e.g., pllimit for limit, plfilterredir for filtering redirects)

## Returns:
    PagesDict: Dictionary mapping link titles to WikipediaPage objects representing the linked pages. Returns empty dict if page doesn't exist (pageid = -1).

## Raises:
    None explicitly raised - relies on underlying _query method for network/HTTP errors

## State Changes:
    Attributes READ: 
        - page.title (to construct API query)
    Attributes WRITTEN:
        - page._attributes["pageid"] (when page doesn't exist, set to -1)
        - page._links (populated with retrieved links)

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
        - The page parameter must be a valid WikipediaPage instance
        - The Wikipedia instance must be properly initialized with a valid user agent
        - The page title must be a valid Wikipedia page title
    Postconditions:
        - The page's _links attribute will be populated with the retrieved links
        - If the page doesn't exist, pageid will be set to -1 and empty dict returned
        - The method will handle pagination automatically for large result sets

## Side Effects:
    - Makes HTTP requests to Wikimedia API endpoints (https://[language].wikipedia.org/w/api.php)
    - Modifies the page object's internal state by updating _links and _attributes
    - Logs API request URLs for debugging purposes

wikipediaapi.__init__.namespace2int.documentation:
## Summary:
Converts a namespace identifier into its corresponding integer value.

## Description:
This utility function standardizes namespace representations by converting either a Namespace enum instance or an integer namespace identifier into a canonical integer format. It serves as a bridge between different namespace representation formats used throughout the Wikipedia API client.

The function is extracted into its own utility function to provide a consistent interface for handling namespace identifiers, ensuring that downstream code can reliably work with integer namespace values regardless of how they were originally provided.

## Args:
    namespace (WikiNamespace): A namespace identifier that may be either a Namespace enum instance or an integer representing a Wikipedia namespace.

## Returns:
    int: The integer value of the namespace. If the input is already an integer, it is returned unchanged. If the input is a Namespace enum instance, its .value attribute is returned.

## Raises:
    None explicitly raised by this function, though invalid inputs could cause unexpected behavior.

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
    - Input must be compatible with the function's logic (either a Namespace enum instance or an integer)
    - When input is a Namespace enum, it must be a valid enum member
    
    Postconditions:
    - Output is always an integer representing the namespace value
    - The function provides consistent integer representation for namespace identifiers

## Side Effects:
    None

## Control Flow:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Input namespace] --> B{Is instance of Namespace?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Return namespace.value]
    B -- No --> D[Return namespace]
    C --> E[Output integer]
    D --> E
```

## Examples:
    # Converting a Namespace enum to integer
    >>> namespace2int(Namespace.MAIN)
    0
    
    # Passing an integer directly
    >>> namespace2int(6)
    6
```

wikipediaapi.__init__.WikipediaPage.summ.documentation:
## Summary
Returns the summary text of the current Wikipedia page by fetching and caching extract data when needed.

## Description
This property provides access to the summary text of a Wikipedia page. It implements lazy loading behavior - the summary data is only fetched from the Wikipedia API when first accessed, and subsequently cached for future accesses. This approach optimizes performance by avoiding unnecessary API calls when the summary isn't needed.

The method checks if the "extracts" data has already been fetched (`self._called["extracts"]`). If not, it calls `self._fetch("extracts")` to retrieve the data from the Wikipedia API, which populates `self._summary` and other related attributes. Finally, it returns the cached summary text stored in `self._summary`.

This method is part of the WikipediaPage class's property-based interface for accessing page content, alongside other properties like `sections`, `text`, and various metadata properties.

## Args
None

## Returns
str: The summary text of the Wikipedia page. Returns an empty string if the page doesn't exist or if there's an error retrieving the summary.

## Raises
None explicitly raised, but may propagate exceptions from underlying API calls via `_fetch` and `_build_extracts` methods.

## State Changes
Attributes READ: 
- `self._called["extracts"]` - checks if extracts data has been fetched
- `self._summary` - returns the cached summary text

Attributes WRITTEN:
- `self._called["extracts"]` - set to True after successful fetch

## Constraints
Preconditions:
- The WikipediaPage instance must be properly initialized with a valid Wikipedia connection
- The page title must be valid (though existence is not required for this property)

Postconditions:
- After first access, `self._called["extracts"]` will be True
- The returned string will contain the page summary text or be empty if no summary exists

## Side Effects
- Makes an HTTP request to the Wikipedia API when first accessed (via `_fetch` method)
- Modifies internal state by setting `self._called["extracts"]` to True after first fetch
- May trigger additional API calls through the `_fetch` method chain

## Summary
Populates common page attributes from API response data into the page's attribute store.

## Description
This static method extracts common page metadata (title, page ID, namespace, redirects) from API response dictionaries and stores them in the page's internal attribute dictionary. It serves as a reusable utility for standardizing attribute population across different Wikipedia API response processing methods.

The method is called by various API processing methods in the Wikipedia class to ensure consistent handling of common page metadata regardless of which API endpoint was used to retrieve the data.

## Args
    extract (dict): API response data containing potential attribute keys
    page (WikipediaPage): Page object whose _attributes dictionary will be updated

## Returns
    None: This method modifies the page object in-place and returns nothing

## Raises
    None: This method does not explicitly raise exceptions

## State Changes
    Attributes READ: None (reads from extract parameter)
    Attributes WRITTEN: Updates page._attributes dictionary with keys: "title", "pageid", "ns", "redirects"

## Constraints
    Preconditions: 
    - extract parameter must be a dictionary-like object that supports the 'in' operator
    - page parameter must be a WikipediaPage instance with a _attributes dictionary
    - The extract dictionary may or may not contain any of the common attributes
    
    Postconditions:
    - Any common attributes present in extract will be copied to page._attributes
    - page._attributes will contain the common attributes if they were present in extract
    - No other attributes in page._attributes will be modified by this method

## Side Effects
    None: This method only modifies the internal state of the provided page object and does not perform I/O operations or external service calls.

## Summary:
Creates a WikipediaPageSection object from a regex match result, handling both wiki and HTML format section parsing.

## Description:
This method constructs a WikipediaPageSection object from a regex match that identifies section headers in extracted Wikipedia content. It processes section information differently based on the extraction format (WIKI vs HTML) by interpreting the appropriate capture groups from the regex match. This method is called during the extraction process when parsing section headers from Wikipedia content.

The method separates the section creation logic from inline code to improve maintainability and testability, and to handle the format-specific parsing requirements cleanly.

## Args:
    match: A regex match object containing section header information from the extracted Wikipedia content. The match object contains capture groups that vary based on the extract_format:
        - For WIKI format: group(1) contains the section heading markup (e.g., "===", group(2) contains the section title)
        - For HTML format: group(1) contains the heading level (e.g., "2"), group(5) contains the section title

## Returns:
    WikipediaPageSection: A newly created section object with appropriate title and level. The section level is adjusted by subtracting 1 from the raw level to normalize the hierarchy.

## Raises:
    None explicitly raised, though ValueError could occur if group(1) conversion to int fails in HTML format (but this would be prevented by the regex pattern)

## State Changes:
    Attributes READ: self.extract_format
    Attributes WRITTEN: None

## Constraints:
    Preconditions: 
    - self.extract_format must be either ExtractFormat.WIKI or ExtractFormat.HTML
    - match must contain the appropriate capture groups for the current extract_format
    - match.group(2) must exist when extract_format is WIKI (for section title)
    - match.group(5) must exist when extract_format is HTML (for section title)
    - match.group(1) must be convertible to integer when extract_format is HTML
    
    Postconditions:
    - Returns a valid WikipediaPageSection instance
    - Section title is properly extracted and stripped
    - Section level is correctly calculated (adjusted by subtracting 1)

## Side Effects:
    None

wikipediaapi.__init__.Wikipedia._query.documentation:
## Summary:
Queries the Wikimedia API to fetch content for a specific Wikipedia page using provided parameters.

## Description:
This private method serves as the core HTTP client for making API requests to Wikimedia services. It constructs the appropriate API endpoint URL using the page's language, adds required parameters (format=json and redirects=1), and executes the HTTP GET request using the session established in the Wikipedia instance. The method is called internally by various public methods such as `extracts`, `info`, `langlinks`, `links`, `backlinks`, `categories`, and `categorymembers` to retrieve different types of information about Wikipedia pages.

## Args:
    page (WikipediaPage): The Wikipedia page object containing language information used to construct the API endpoint URL.
    params (Dict[str, Any]): Dictionary of API parameters to be sent with the request.

## Returns:
    dict: JSON response from the Wikimedia API parsed as a Python dictionary.

## Raises:
    requests.RequestException: If the HTTP request fails due to network issues, timeouts, or invalid responses.
    KeyError: If the API response doesn't contain expected keys (though this would be rare with proper API usage).

## State Changes:
    Attributes READ: 
        - self._session: Used to execute the HTTP GET request
        - self._request_kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to the HTTP request
        - page.language: Used to construct the base API URL
    
    Attributes WRITTEN: None

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
        - The Wikipedia instance must have a valid session established (self._session should not be None)
        - The page parameter must be a valid WikipediaPage object with a language attribute
        - The params dictionary should contain valid Wikimedia API parameters
        
    Postconditions:
        - The returned dictionary contains the parsed JSON response from the Wikimedia API
        - The HTTP request is executed with proper formatting and redirect handling

## Side Effects:
    - Makes an HTTP GET request to Wikimedia API endpoints
    - Logs the constructed request URL at INFO level
    - May cause network I/O delays depending on API response times

## Summary:
Retrieves language links for a Wikipedia page from the Wikimedia API and populates the page's language link attributes.

## Description:
This method fetches inter-language links for a given Wikipedia page by making an API call to Wikimedia. It processes the API response to build a dictionary of language links, where keys are language codes and values are corresponding WikipediaPage objects. The method handles special cases like non-existent pages and updates common page attributes.

This method is typically called internally through the WikipediaPage.langlinks property getter, which triggers the lazy loading mechanism when language links are first accessed.

## Args:
    page (WikipediaPage): The Wikipedia page object for which to retrieve language links
    **kwargs: Additional parameters to pass to the Wikimedia API query (e.g., lllimit for limit number of results)

## Returns:
    Dict[str, WikipediaPage]: Dictionary mapping language codes to WikipediaPage objects representing the page in different languages, or empty dict if page doesn't exist

## Raises:
    None explicitly raised - relies on underlying API calls and internal methods

## State Changes:
    Attributes READ: 
        - page.title
        - page.language
    
    Attributes WRITTEN:
        - page._langlinks (populated with language link data)
        - page._attributes (updated with pageid, title, ns, redirects metadata)

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
        - The page parameter must be a valid WikipediaPage instance
        - The page must have a valid title attribute
        - The page must have a valid language attribute
    
    Postconditions:
        - The page's _langlinks attribute is populated with language link data
        - The page's _attributes dictionary contains updated metadata
        - If the page doesn't exist (API returns -1), pageid is set to -1 and empty dict is returned

## Side Effects:
    - Makes HTTP GET request to Wikimedia API endpoint
    - Updates the page object's internal state (attributes and langlinks)
    - Uses the session object for making network requests

example.print_categorymembers.documentation:
## Summary:
Prints a hierarchical representation of Wikipedia categories with indentation showing nesting levels.

## Description:
This function recursively traverses and displays Wikipedia category hierarchies, showing the relationship between parent and child categories. It prints each category with appropriate indentation based on its nesting level and only recurses into subcategories to avoid infinite loops in deeply nested structures.

## Args:
    categorymembers (dict-like): Dictionary containing Wikipedia category objects with title, namespace, and categorymembers attributes
    level (int): Current nesting level for indentation (default: 0)
    max_level (int): Maximum recursion depth to prevent excessive nesting (default: 2)

## Returns:
    None: This function performs I/O operations and does not return any value

## Raises:
    AttributeError: If categorymembers items don't have required attributes (title, ns, categorymembers)
    RecursionError: If max_level is set too high and category hierarchy is very deep

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
        - categorymembers must be iterable with .values() method
        - Each item in categorymembers must have title, ns, and categorymembers attributes
        - level and max_level must be non-negative integers
    
    Postconditions:
        - All categories up to max_level will be printed with proper indentation
        - Function terminates when max_level is reached or no more categories exist

## Side Effects:
    - Prints formatted output to standard output (stdout)
    - No external state mutations or I/O operations beyond console output

## Control Flow:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Start print_categorymembers] --> B{categorymembers not empty?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Iterate categorymembers]
    C --> D[Print current category with indentation]
    D --> E{Current item is category?}
    E -- Yes --> F{Level < Max Level?}
    F -- Yes --> G[Recursive call with level+1]
    F -- No --> H[Continue iteration]
    E -- No --> H
    H --> I[Next category]
    I --> J{More categories?}
    J -- Yes --> C
    J -- No --> K[End]
    B -- No --> K
```

## Examples:
```python
# Basic usage
print_categorymembers(categories_dict)

# With custom depth limit
print_categorymembers(categories_dict, max_level=3)
```

## Summary:
Recursively prints hierarchical Wikipedia article sections with indentation and title-text information.

## Description:
Recursively prints Wikipedia article sections with increasing indentation levels, displaying section titles and first 40 characters of section text. This function separates presentation logic from data extraction, allowing article processing pipelines to focus on content retrieval while delegating display concerns to this utility function.

## Args:
    sections (list): List of section objects with 'title' and 'text' attributes
    level (int): Current nesting level for indentation calculation, defaults to 0

## Returns:
    None: This function performs I/O operations but returns no value

## Raises:
    AttributeError: If any section object lacks 'title' or 'text' attributes
    TypeError: If sections parameter is not iterable or contains non-section objects

## Constraints:
    Preconditions:
    - sections parameter must be iterable (list-like structure)
    - Each section object must have 'title' and 'text' string attributes
    - level parameter must be a non-negative integer
    
    Postconditions:
    - All sections and their subsections are printed to standard output
    - Function terminates when all sections are processed

## Side Effects:
    - Writes formatted text to standard output (stdout)
    - No external state mutations or I/O beyond console output

## Control Flow:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[print_sections called] --> B{sections empty?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Return]
    B -- No --> D[Print current section info]
    D --> E[Increment level]
    E --> F[Recursive call print_sections]
    F --> G[Loop to next section]
    G --> B
```

## Examples:
```python
# Basic usage with Wikipedia sections
sections = wiki_page.sections  # From wikipediaapi
print_sections(sections)

# With custom starting level
print_sections(sections, level=1)
```

## WikipediaPage Class

### Summary:
The `WikipediaPage` class represents a single Wikipedia article and provides access to its content, metadata, and related information. It implements a lazy-loading mechanism to efficiently fetch data only when needed.

### Description:
This class serves as the primary interface for working with individual Wikipedia articles. It stores page metadata and provides properties that automatically trigger API calls to fetch content when accessed for the first time. The class supports navigation through article sections, links to other pages, and access to various metadata fields.

### Key Responsibilities:
- Stores and manages page metadata (title, language, namespace, etc.)
- Implements lazy loading for content retrieval
- Provides access to article text, sections, and structured content
- Manages relationships with linked pages (links, categories, language links)
- Handles attribute access with automatic API calls when needed

### Core Properties:
1. **`title`** - Title of the Wikipedia page
2. **`language`** - Language code of the Wikipedia edition
3. **`namespace`** - Namespace identifier for the page
4. **`summary`** - Article summary text
5. **`text`** - Full article text including sections
6. **`sections`** - List of article sections with hierarchy
7. **`links`** - Pages linked from this article
8. **`categories`** - Categories this article belongs to
9. **`langlinks`** - Links to equivalent articles in other languages
10. **`backlinks`** - Pages that link to this article

### Implementation Details:
- Uses `__getattr__` magic method for lazy loading of attributes
- Maintains `_called` dictionary to track which API calls have been made
- Stores content in private attributes (`_summary`, `_section`, etc.)
- Implements `_fetch` method to trigger API calls for specific data types
- Supports section navigation via `_section_mapping` dictionary

### Usage Example:
```python
import wikipediaapi

wiki = wikipediaapi.Wikipedia('MyApp/1.0')
page = wiki.page('Python_(programming_language)')

# These will trigger API calls only when accessed
summary = page.summary  # Fetches extracts
links = page.links      # Fetches links
sections = page.sections # Fetches extracts with section structure
```
